questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Argon and chlorine are elements in the same period of the Periodic Table.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Draw the electron diagram for a molecule of argon, showing electrons in all shells.
sub_questions.answer: [IMG:ans_q1_a_0.jpg]

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: What is the type of intermolecular force in chlorine gas?
sub_questions.answer: Van der Waals' forces

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text:
Complete the table below by stating the natural source and the method of extraction from the source for each element.
ElementNatural sourceMethod of extraction
Argon
Chlorine
sub_questions.answer:
ElementNatural sourceMethod of extraction
ArgonThe atmosphere / AirFractional distillation of liquefied air
ChlorineRock salt / Sea water / OceanElectrolysis of sea water

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text: For each of the following experiments, state the expected observation, and write the chemical equation(s) for the reaction(s) involved.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: passing carbon dioxide gas into limewater until in excess
sub_questions.answer:
A white precipitate is firstly formed, the precipitate dissolves in the presence of excess  \( CO_{2}(g) \) . 
\( \mathrm{Ca(OH)_{2}(aq) + CO_{2}(g) \rightarrow CaCO_{3}(s) + H_{2}O(l)} \)  
\( \mathrm{CaCO_{3}(s) + CO_{2}(g) + H_{2}O(l) \rightarrow Ca(HCO_{3})_{2}(aq)} \)

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: adding sodium sulphite solution to acidified potassium dichromate solution until in excess
sub_questions.answer:
The solution changes from orange to green.
\( Cr_{2}O_{7}^{2-}(aq) + 3SO_{3}^{2-}(aq) + 8H^{+}(aq) \rightarrow 2Cr^{3+}(aq) + 3SO_{4}^{2-}(aq) + 4H_{2}O(l) \)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Aluminium and iron are commonly used construction materials.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest why iron was used earlier than aluminium in history.
sub_questions.answer: Iron is less reactive than aluminium.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: A compound contains iron and oxygen only. In an experiment for determining the empirical formula of this compound, 2.31 g of the compound was heated with carbon monoxide. Upon complete reaction, carbon dioxide and 1.67 g of iron were formed.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Calculate the empirical formula of this compound.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Write the chemical equation for the reaction involved in the experiment.

children.label: iii
children.question_text: As carbon monoxide is poisonous, suggest one necessary safety precaution in carrying out the experiment.
children.answer: Perform the experiment in a fume cupboard.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why a galvanised iron object does not easily rust even if the zinc layer is broken.
sub_questions.answer:
Zn is more reactive / a stronger reducing agent than Fe.
For galvanised iron object with the zinc layer broken, iron will be protected from corrosion as zinc will be preferentially oxidised.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why anodisation can prevent aluminium objects from corrosion.
sub_questions.answer:
The surface of the aluminium object is oxidised to  \( Al_{2}O_{3}(s) \)  / aluminium oxide. 1
\( Al_{2}O_{3}(s) \)  is impermeable to water / oxygen, thus corrosion of aluminium is inhibited. 1

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text: Lead-acid accumulator is a secondary cell containing sulphuric acid. It is commonly used in starting up motor vehicle engines.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: What is meant by the term ‘secondary cell’?
sub_questions.answer: A cell that can be recharged.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Suggest why a lead-acid accumulator is suitable for starting up motor vehicle engines.
sub_questions.answer: It can provide a high current / voltage / power to start up the engine.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: State one environmental impact that would be imposed from the disposal of lead-acid accumulators.
sub_questions.answer: Lead / Lead compounds are toxic. / Sulphuric acid is corrosive / irritant.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: A student diluted a sample of concentrated sulphuric acid for making a lead-acid accumulator.

children.label: i
children.question_text: Describe how concentrated sulphuric acid can be diluted in a laboratory. State a safety precaution needed during the dilution process.
children.answer:
Pour a small amount of the concentrated sulphuric acid to a large amount of water. 
Wear goggle / face shield / safety spectacles / safety glasses / gloves.

children.label: ii
children.question_text:
5.00 cm³ of the solution in the lead-acid accumulator made contains 2.48 g of sulphuric acid. Calculate the molarity of the sulphuric acid in the solution. 
(Molar mass of sulphuric acid = 98.1 g)
children.answer:
Number of mole of sulphuric acid = 2.48 / 98.1 = 0.0253
Molarity of sulphuric acid = 0.0253 / 0.005 = 5.06 (M)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Explain, with the aid of a chemical equation, why  \( \mathrm{NH}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \)  is regarded as a weak alkali. Suggest how you would show that  \( \mathrm{NH}_{3}(\mathrm{aq}) \)  is a weaker alkali than  \( \mathrm{NaOH}(\mathrm{aq}) \)  through an experiment.
questions.answer:
• Equation:  \( NH_{3} + H_{2}O \rightleftharpoons NH_{4}^{+} + OH^{-} \)
• Explanation: Ammonia ionises / dissociates slightly in water. / The ionisation / dissociation of ammonia in water is incomplete.
• Method: Measure respectively the pH / electrical conductivity / enthalpy change of neutralisation / temperature rise in neutralisation of both  \( NH_{3}(aq) \)  and NaOH(aq).
• Observation: pH / electrical conductivity / enthalpy change of neutralisation / temperature rise in neutralisation of  \( NH_{3}(aq) \)  is lower than that of NaOH(aq).
• Fair comparison:
pH – same concentration of  \( NH_{3}(aq) \)  and NaOH(aq)
electrical conductivity – same concentration of  \( NH_{3}(aq) \)  and NaOH(aq)
enthalpy change of neutralisation – same amount of  \( NH_{3}(aq) \)  and NaOH(aq)
temperature rise in neutralisation – same volume and concentration of  \( NH_{3}(aq) \)  and NaOH(aq)
• Communication mark

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 5: Fossil Fuels and Carbon Compounds
questions.question_text:
The steps involved in the reaction of methane with bromine forming  \( CH_{3}Br \)  can be shown by the following diagram. Only electrons in the outermost shells are shown.

[IMG:q6_img_0.jpg]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Name the type of the reaction for the formation of  \( CH_{3}Br \)  from methane and bromine.
sub_questions.answer: substitution reaction

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: State the condition needed for the reaction to occur.
sub_questions.answer: Light / hν / ultra-violet / UV / heat / radical initiator

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: State the expected observation for the reaction.
sub_questions.answer:
Orange / brown colour fades away. /
Orange / brown colour changes to colourless slowly.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: With reference to its electronic structure, explain why the species Br has a high reactivity.
sub_questions.answer:
Br atom does not have the stable noble gas electronic configuration. /
Br atom does not have the stable octet electronic configuration. /
The electronic configuration of Br atom does not fulfill the octet rule.

sub_questions.label: e
sub_questions.question_text: The reaction of methane with bromine can also form other single-carbon-containing organic compounds.

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Suggest a condition so that the reaction of methane with bromine can form more  \( CH_{3}Br \)  but less other organic compounds.
children.answer:
Use large excess amount of  \( CH_{4} \) . /
\( Br_{2} \)  is the limiting reactant.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 7: Redox Reactions, Chemical Cells and Electrolysis
questions.question_text:
Refer to the set-up for electroplating an object shown in the diagram below.

[IMG:q7_img_0.jpg]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why oily dirt on the object should be removed before electroplating.
sub_questions.answer: Oily dirt hinders the conduction of electricity / hinders the plating of copper on the object.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Copper(II) sulphate is an electrolyte. What is meant by the term ‘electrolyte’?
sub_questions.answer:
Electrolyte is a compound that conducts electricity when melted or dissolved in water. / 
Electrolyte is a substance that consists of mobile ions when melted or dissolved in water. / 
Electrolyte is a substance that undergoes decomposition when electricity is passing through it.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: List ALL the ions existing in the solution.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why copper(II) ions are preferentially discharged during the electroplating process.
sub_questions.answer:
Copper(II) ion has higher oxidising power than hydrogen ion. / 
Copper(II) ion undergoes reduction more readily than hydrogen ion.

sub_questions.label: e
sub_questions.question_text: Write the half equation of the change that occurs at the anode.
sub_questions.answer: \( Cu \rightarrow Cu^{2+} + 2e^{-} \)

sub_questions.label: f
sub_questions.question_text: State the observable change, if any, in the solution during the electroplating process.
sub_questions.answer: No observable change

sub_questions.label: g
sub_questions.question_text:
It is known that  \( 2.28 \times 10^{22} \)  electrons have passed through the external circuit during the electroplating process. Calculate the mass of copper that would theoretically be plated on the object.

(Relative atomic mass: Cu = 63.5; Avogadro's constant =  \( 6.02 \times 10^{23} \)  mol \( ^{-1} \) )
sub_questions.answer:
Number of mole of electrons involved =  \( 2.28 \times 10^{22} / 6.02 \times 10^{23} = 0.0379 \)  
Mass of copper formed =  \( 0.0379 \times 63.5 / 2 = 1.20 \)  (g)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Natural gas is an important energy source for electricity generation. It contains mainly methane (CH_{4}).

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: Write the general formula of the molecules in the homologous series that methane belongs to.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text:
The combustion of methane is an exothermic reaction. Its chemical equation is shown below :

\(  CH_{4}(g)+2O_{2}(g)\rightarrow CO_{2}(g)+2H_{2}O(l) \)

children.label: i
children.question_text:
Complete the table below by stating all the covalent bond(s) that are broken and formed during the combustion of methane.
Covalent bond(s) broken
Covalent bond(s) formed
children.answer:
Covalent bond(s) brokenC-H and O=O
Covalent bond(s) formedC=O and H-O

children.label: ii
children.question_text: Suggest why the combustion is exothermic in terms of the breaking and forming of covalent bonds.
children.answer: The total energy released in the bond forming process is larger than the total energy absorbed in the bond breaking process.

children.label: iii
children.question_text:
Calculate the standard enthalpy change of combustion of methane.
(Standard enthalpy changes of formation :
\( CH_{4}(g) = -74.8 \, kJ \, mol^{-1} \) ;  \( CO_{2}(g) = -393.5 \, kJ \, mol^{-1} \) ;  \( H_{2}O(l) = -285.9 \, kJ \, mol^{-1} \) )
children.answer: \( \begin{array}{r l r l}&{\mathrm{}}&{\Delta H_{\mathrm{e}}^{\theta}=\Delta H_{\mathrm{f}}^{\theta}\left[\mathrm{C O}_{2}(\mathrm{g})\right]+2\Delta H_{\mathrm{f}}^{\theta}\left[\mathrm{H}_{2}\mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})\right]-\Delta H_{\mathrm{f}}^{\theta}\left[\mathrm{C H}_{4}(\mathrm{g})\right]}\\ &{}&{=\left(-393.5\right)+2(-285.9)-\left(-74.8\right)}\\ &{}&{=-890.5\left(\mathrm{k J}\mathrm{m o l}^{-1}\right)}\end{array} \quad \)

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Some regions tend to generate electricity more by natural gas but less by coal. Give TWO reasons from environmental protection consideration.
sub_questions.answer:
• Natural gas burns more completely but coal does not. / Burning coal would produce soot / carbon monoxide but burning natural gas would not.
• Compared with natural gas, coal contains more impurities. / Burning coal would produce more pollutants, such as  \( SO_{2} \) , metal compound dust.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Consider the reaction below :

\(methyl oleate\)
\(  CH_{3}(CH_{2})_{7}CH=CH(CH_{2})_{7}CO_{2}CH_{3}(l) + H_{2}(g) \xrightarrow{\text{catalyst}} CH_{3}(CH_{2})_{7}CH_{2}CH_{2}(CH_{2})_{7}CO_{2}CH_{3}(l) \)

At room temperature and pressure, a micro-scale experiment was performed using the set-up shown below in which 0.080 g of methyl oleate in an organic solvent was allowed to react with excess  \( H_{2}(g) \) . The  \( H_{2}(g) \)  flowed from the inverted measuring cylinder to the reacting flask through the tubing.

[IMG:q9_img_0.jpg]

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: State one advantage of conducting this reaction in a micro-scale experiment.
sub_questions.answer: save cost on chemicals / minimise chemical hazards / save time on carrying out experiment / reduce the consumption of chemicals / reduce chemical waste

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Explain why the right end of the tubing was placed at the uppermost position of the inverted measuring cylinder.
sub_questions.answer:
Prevent sucking back of water. /
Prevent water from entering the reacting flask.

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: State an expected observation in the inverted measuring cylinder during the reaction.
sub_questions.answer:
Water level inside the measuring cylinder rises. /
The gas volume inside the measuring cylinder reduces.

sub_questions.label: d
sub_questions.answer:
Number of moles of methyl oleate used \(= 0.08\ /\ 296 = 2.70 \times 10^{-4}\)
Minimum volume of H_{2}(g) required \(= (0.08 / 296)\times24000 cm^{3} = 6.49\ cm^{3}\)

children.label: i
children.question_text: Sketch, in the graph below, the variation of the volume of  \( H_{2}(g) \)  in the measuring cylinder with time from start until the completion of the reaction. You should label this sketch as 'A'. (The measuring cylinder initially contained  \( 10.0 \, cm^{3} \)  of  \( H_{2}(g) \) . The first few points have been given in the graph to facilitate the sketch.)

children.label: ii
children.question_text: In the same graph above, give another sketch as required in (i) but only using 0.040 g of methyl oleate for the reaction while the other conditions remain unchanged. You should label this sketch as 'B'.

questions.subject: Chemistry
topics: Topic 12: Patterns in the Chemical World

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: For each of the oxides below, draw its electron diagram (showing electrons in the outermost shells only), and state its behaviour in water.

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: Using iron as an example, illustrate TWO characteristics of transition metals.
sub_questions.answer:
Any TWO of the following:
• Fe can have variable oxidation numbers – Fe^{2+}, Fe^{3+}
• Fe can act as a catalyst – e.g., Fe in Haber Process
• Fe forms coloured compounds – Fe^{2+}(aq) is green / Fe^{3+}(aq) is yellow

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
Refer to the following chemical equation :

\( \mathrm{H_{2}O(l)\rightleftharpoons H^{+}(aq)+OH^{-}(aq)}\qquad\Delta H>0 \)

Under fixed conditions,  \( [H_{2}O(l)] \)  is considered as a constant. In consideration of the definition of  \( K_{c} \) ,  \( [H^{+}(aq)][OH^{-}(aq)] \)  would also be a constant.

sub_questions.label: a
sub_questions.question_text: The pH of an aqueous solution is defined as  \( -\log[H^{+}(aq)] \) . The pH of water equals 7.0 at 298 K. Find, at this temperature, the :

sub_questions.label: b
sub_questions.question_text: \( [H_{2}O(l)] \)  equals 55.6 mol dm \( ^{-3} \)  at 298 K. Suggest why  \( [H_{2}O(l)] \)  is considered as a constant with reference to the values of  \( [H^{+}(aq)] \)  and  \( [OH^{-}(aq)] \) .

sub_questions.label: c
sub_questions.question_text: Explain whether the pH of water at 328 K would be less than 7.0, equal to 7.0, or greater than 7.0.
sub_questions.answer:
The pH of water would be less than 7.
The dissociation of  \( H_{2}O(l) \)  is endothermic. Increasing the temperature will shift the equilibrium position to the right.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text:
You are provided with 
[IMG:q12_img_0.jpg], 
inorganic reagents and organic solvents.
Outline a synthetic route, with no more than three steps, to obtain the following compound :

[IMG:q12_img_1.jpg]

For each step, give the reagent(s), reaction conditions (as appropriate) and structure of the organic product.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.question_text: Using  \( C_{2}H_{5}CH(OH)CH_{3} \)  as an example, write a paragraph to illustrate ‘enantiomerism’. Suitable diagram(s) should be included in your answer.
questions.answer:
[IMG:ans_q13_0.jpg]

• Chiral centre
• Non-superimposable on its mirror image
• Optically active
• Communication mark
